steps.title: Read the claim precisely
steps.content: The claim is about Dr. Proudie's exaggerated sense of his own abilities. The quotation must show Proudie overrating himself, not merely show the narrator criticizing him, other people noticing him, or a neutral description of his usefulness.

steps.title: Spot the self-flattery
steps.content: B says Proudie fondly flattered himself that he had gifts that would bring him higher notice, which directly shows an inflated self-image. A is the narrator's deflating judgment of his abilities. C lists outside reasons people in power noticed him. D describes limited practical tact, not Proudie's own exaggerated belief about himself. The answer is B.
